TAMIL  NADU
GOVERNMENT GAZETTE
PUBLISHED  BY  AUTHORITY
No.  2A] 
CHENNAI, WEDNESDAY, JANUARY 11, 2017 
 
 
 
 
  
Margazhi 27, Thunmugi, Thiruvalluvar Aandu – 2047
Part II—Section  2
(Supplement)
NOTIFICATIONS  BY  GOVERNMENT
AGRICULTURE DEPARTMENT
Secretariat, 11th January 2017
Scheme of Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana during Rabi 2016-2017 Season.
No. II(2)/AG/30/2017.
[AGRICULTURE - NOTIFICATION OF CROPS, DISTRICTS, FIRKAS AND REVENUE VILLAGES FOR 
AGRICULTURE CROPS & HORTICULTURE CROPS AND PUBLICATION IN THE STATE GAZETTE 
UNDER THE SCHEME OF PRADHAN MANTRI FASAL BIMA YOJANA DURING RABI 2016-17 
SEASON - ORDERS ISSUED.]
The following Government Order is published:-- 
[G.O. (Rt.) No. 301, Agriculture (AP6), 21st September 2016,
¹ó†ì£C 5, ¶¡ºA, F¼õœÀõ˜ Ý‡´-2047.]
READ:
1. 
From the Joint Secretary, Government of India, Department of Agriculture Co-operation & Farmers Welfare, 
Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare, New Delhi, Letter F.No. 13015/03/2016-Credit II, dated 
23-02-2016.
2. 
G.O.(Ms.) No. 220, Agriculture (AP6) Department, dated 21-07-2016.
3. 
From the Commissioner of Agriculture Letter, No. G1/7976/2016, dated 07-09-2016.
 
 Order : G.O. (Rt.) No. 301, Agriculture (AP6), 21st September 2016.
In the letter ﬁ rst read above the Government of India have introduced a New Crop Insurance Scheme "Pradhan 
Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana" (PMFBY) by replacing the "National Agricultural Insurance Scheme (NAIS) and have Issued 
administrative approval for implementation of the scheme in all the States of India from Kharif 2016 onwards following 
the guidelines of the new scheme Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana. 
2. The 36th State Level Co-ordination Committee meeting was held on 29-03-2016 and the modalities of implementation 
of Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana in Tamil Nadu were discussed with the members of the committee on the 
background of the operational guidelines framed and communicated by Government of India for implementation in the 
State of Tamil Nadu. 
 
© 
[Regd. No. TN/CCN/467/2012-14.
 GOVERNMENT  OF  TAMIL  NADU 
[R. Dis. No. 197/2009. 
 
 
 
2017

DTP—II-2—Sup.(2A)–1


2
3, In the Government Order second read above, orders have been issued according administrative sanction for the 
implementation of Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana in all the districts of Tamil Nadu (except Chennai) from Kharif 
2016 onwards,
4. In the letter third read above, the Commissioner of Agriculture has Informed", that the 31 districts of Tamil Nadu 
(except Chennai) are formed into three clusters by combination of 3 high risk districts, 2 medium risk districts and 5-6 
low risk districts as follows:-

Indemnity Level for the purpose of calculation of Threshold yield is ﬁ xed at 90% for low  risk districts, 80% for 
medium risk districts and 70% for high risk districts. The insurance companies selected as implementing agency for the 
three clusters are as  follows:-

5. The Commissioner of Agriculture has also furnished the details of the scheme, premium rate applicable for 
farmers, the localized perils notiﬁ ed in the districts, premium rate approved for the crops notiﬁ ed in all the districts, the 
district wise, crop wise details of notiﬁ ed insurance units etc., and has requested the Government to issue orders on 
the following: 
i. 
Approval of the Implementing Agency (Insurance Company) for implementation of the insurance companies 
in the three clusters. 
II.  For notiﬁ cation of Premium Rates approved for Rabi 2016-17 season. 
iii.  Notiﬁ cation of the districts under localized perils. 
iv.  Notiﬁ cation of insurance units which are eligible for enrolment of farmers under Pradhan Mantri Fasal 
Bima Yojana.
6. The Government after careful examination accept the proposal of the Commissioner of Agriculture. Accordingly, 
the Government hereby notify the Crops Districts, Firkas and Revenue Villages for AgricuIture Crops & Horticulture 
Crops Implementing Agencies, premium rates approved, districts under localized perils and the insurance units which 
are eligible for enrolment of farmers as shown In the Annexure I, II & III to this order for the effective implementation 
of the Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana during Rabi 2016-17 season. 
7. The claims under the Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana Scheme shall be settled on the basis of yield data 
furnished by the Director, Department of Economics and Statistics as per prescribed cut-off dates for the notiﬁ ed crops 
districts/ ﬁ rkas for the effective implementation of Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana scheme for Rabi 2016 season and 
not on the basis of the Annavari Certiﬁ cate / Gazette notiﬁ cation declaring the area as Drought/Flood/Cyclone affected 
etc. by the District Collectors.
By order of the Governor


3
ANNEXURE I 
[G.O.(Rt) No.301, Agriculture (AP6) Department, Dated 21.09.2016] 
Notiﬁ cation of Crops, Districts, Firkas and Revenue villages for Rabi 2016-17
 Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY) will be implemented in all districts of Tamil Nadu (except Chennai) 
with the state Government subsidy during Rabi 2016-17. 
I. Objectives of PMFBY: 
 
PMFBY aims at supporting sustainable production in agriculture by way of 
 
a. Providing ﬁ nancial support to farmers suffering crop loss/damage arising out of unforeseen events. 
 
b. Stabilizing the income of farmers to ensure their continuance in farming. 
 
c. Encouraging farmers to adopt innovative and modern Agriculture practices. 
 
d. Ensuring ﬂ ow of credit to the agriculture sector, which will, contribute to food security, crop diversiﬁ cation and 
enhancing growth and competitiveness of agriculture sector besides protectlng farmers from production risks. 
II.  Coverage of farmers: 
 
All farmers including share croppers and tenant farmers growing the notiﬁ ed crops in the notiﬁ ed areas are eligible 
for coverage. 
 
• 
Compulsory to loanee farmers. 
 
• 
Optional for non loanee farmers. 
Ill. Coverage of risks and exclusions: 
1.  Prevented Sowing/Planting Risk - Insured area is prevented from sowing/planting due to deﬁ cit rainfall or adverse 
seasonal conditions and due lead to non release of irrigation water on scheduled date of opening of dam for Irrigation 
in its command areas (GOI Lr No.13015/01/2016· Credit II dated 25.4.16) 
2.  Standing Crop(Sowing to Harvesting) - Comprehensive risk insurance is provided to cover yield losses due to 
non-preventable risks viz., Drought, dry spells, ﬂ ood, inundation, pests and diseases, landslides. natural ﬁ re and lightening, 
storm, hailstorm, cyclone, typhoon, tempest, hurricane and tornado. 
3. Post-Harvest Losses - Coverage is available only up to a maximum period of two weeks from the harvesting 
for those crops which are allowed to dry in cut and spread condition in the ﬁ eld after harvesting against speciﬁ c perils 
of cyclone. cyclonic rains and unseasonal rains. (last date by which harvesting should have been done is mentioned 
in seasonality discipline) 
4. Localized Calamities • Loss/damage resulting from occurrence of identiﬁ ed localized risks of hailstorm, landslide 
and inundation affecting isolated farms in the notiﬁ ed area.
Sl.No.
(1)
Localized Perils
(2)
Districts Notiﬁ ed
(3)
1.

2.

The Nilgiris, Kanyakumari, Salem, Thirunelveli and Thiruchirapalli.
3.
Inundation
All Districts
Post-Harvest losses
4.
Cyclone/Cyclonic rain and unseasonal rains
All Districts
 
IV. Sum Insured:
 
 
Sum Insured for both Loanee and Non Loanee farmers will be the same and equal to the Scale of Finance.
 
V. Premium Rates and Premium Subsidy:
 
Farmers Premium: Actuarial Premium Rate (APR) would be charged under PMFBY by the Implementing Agency 
(IA). The rate of Insurance Charges payable by the farmer will be as per the following table:


4

Crops
(2)
Maximum Insurance Charges payable by farmer
(3)
1.
All Food Grain Crops and Oil Seed Crops (all 
cereals, Millets, Pulses and Oilseed Crops.)
1.5% of Sum insured or Actuarial rate, whichever is less
2.
Annual Commercial/Annual Horticultural Crops
5.0% of Sum insured or Actuarial rate, whichever is less
